page: Amazon Web Services i4g.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture running at 2.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core, and includes 1875 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data shows average performance across several key metrics. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests fall within the middle 50% tier, with scores of 1519.59 and 12132.09 respectively. Memory bandwidth is measured at 472.81 GB/sec for cached read-only small blocks, while uncached large block reads reach 116.33 GB/sec. However, single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show weaker performance, with a poor rating for single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 7.19 GB/sec and a weak rating for single-threaded gzip compression at 20.3 GB/sec.

This instance is configured for workloads requiring local NVMe storage and moderate memory density, such as database operations and static web serving. Passmark database operations scored 5224.61, and Redis SET operations reached 9.8 million operations per second, both placing in the average tier. The ARM64 architecture provides a cost-efficient hardware profile for resource-dense storage tasks, though it exhibits lower performance in cryptographic operations and LLM inference, where text generation for a 7B model is limited to 6.74 tokens per second.
description: Amazon Web Services i4g.2xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It is configured with 8 dedicated physical cores on an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 1875 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate average performance for database operations, with Redis SET operations achieving 9.8 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring 5224.61. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in single-threaded decompression and cryptographic tasks, as well as LLM inference. This hardware profile is suited for storage-intensive workloads, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from local NVMe storage and a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, offering qualitative cost efficiency through its dedicated ARM architecture.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4g.2xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized server with 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 1875 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering average database and Redis performance.

tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring dedicated cores, 64 GB RAM, and local NVMe SSD for database and web serving workloads.
